Serious post:
Had many messages from people who are finding the current environment tough. Theres a few common things: oversizing, use of leverage, not using stops,  holding stuff they borrowed conviction in. 

Size down until get traction , avoid leverage and hold more cash

tweets.text:
This would be a Lighter frontend right? If so incredibly smart, and coupled with fees and the stock Index resistribution function potentially explosive. 

Couple of questions though?

1) couldn’t find https://t.co/IWoZanEQOQ configured fee bps

2) Is the 75/25 routing is enforced by Lighter/integration config or handled by Index’s backend?

3) how are you gonna incentivize trader to use https://t.co/IWoZanEQOQ frontend vs Lighter as I assume fees will have to be slightly higher to feed the flywheel?

Either way, loving to see some real experimentation and my RWA thesis playing out on RH chain, first inmings only. Solazy fumbled a billion dollar bag.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Introducing https://t.co/VIhlNNrJe7  

Trade real world assets with up to 50x leverage.  

Every trade feeds the flywheel: 
→ 75% of fees buy stocks for the treasury, distributed to Index holders. 
→ 25% deepen liquidity.
  
Stocks for everyone. https://t.co/W2kG9N5WQs
tweets.full_text:
This would be a Lighter frontend right? If so incredibly smart, and coupled with fees and the stock Index resistribution function potentially explosive. 

Couple of questions though?

1) couldn’t find https://t.co/IWoZanEQOQ configured fee bps

2) Is the 75/25 routing is enforced by Lighter/integration config or handled by Index’s backend?

3) how are you gonna incentivize trader to use https://t.co/IWoZanEQOQ frontend vs Lighter as I assume fees will have to be slightly higher to feed the flywheel?

Either way, loving to see some real experimentation and my RWA thesis playing out on RH chain, first inmings only. Solazy fumbled a billion dollar bag.

tweets.text:
Been on Fintwit since 2012ish (Stocktwits, X) and I can tell you that there are just some of the smartest and coolest people you will communicate with. Literally so many of ya’ll I bet we can be real friends IRL. Stay positive. Treat people with respect even if behind a monitor and keyboard. You can always pick up on a few things to enhance your life and knowledge and trading and mental. I know I am still very much learning in my journey.

Kthxbye.

tweets.text:
EIA reported -1.7 million bbls for crude, -3 million bbls for SPR. -4.7 million bbls total. 

Total liquids saw a build of 10.3 million bbls as product exports fell by 1.785 million bbls week over week. 

Implied demand for distillate dropped by 25%, which is likely a timing issue. Distillate inventory saw a build of 4.6 million bbls.

thread_tweets.text:
EIA has reported draws below our estimate. Partially attributed to higher than expected US crude oil production, but also related to when those barrels return (storage difference). 

The lag can last 2-4 weeks.

tweets.text: Today, Kevin Warsh was asked specifically what he would do about the inflation problem. The three actions he claimed he would take amount to all talk. Warsh's plan is to convince the markets that he is serious about reducing inflation without actually doing anything to reduce it.
tweets.full_text: Today, Kevin Warsh was asked specifically what he would do about the inflation problem. The three actions he claimed he would take amount to all talk. Warsh's plan is to convince the markets that he is serious about reducing inflation without actually doing anything to reduce it.

tweets.text: Doesn’t it bother you that the current Secretary of the Treasury doesn’t know that the U.S. halted redemption rights on all gold and silver certificates decades ago? If Scott Bessent is this ignorant of a basic historical fact regarding U.S. currency, what else doesn’t he know?
tweets.full_text: Doesn’t it bother you that the current Secretary of the Treasury doesn’t know that the U.S. halted redemption rights on all gold and silver certificates decades ago? If Scott Bessent is this ignorant of a basic historical fact regarding U.S. currency, what else doesn’t he know?

tweets.text: Is the current Secretary of the Treasury really this misinformed? He just said that if you have an old gold or silver certificate, you can bring it to Fort Knox and claim your metal. That’s false. Gold certificates have not been redeemable for gold since 1933, and silver-certificate redemption ended in 1968. If Bessent is this ignorant about the history and status of U.S. currency, why should we trust his assurances about the gold supposedly held at Fort Knox?
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
FORT KNOX GOLD CONFIRMED 🚨

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent says all US gold is present and accounted for.

Over $1 TRILLION in gold.

The largest gold pile in the world.🇺🇸 https://t.co/Hr9LKBPlyd

tweets.text: Of course AI will destroy more jobs than it creates; that’s progress. It means humans won’t have to work as much, as more of what we need will be produced by machines. But for those who still need or want to work, better-paying employment opportunities will become available.
